core:effect.name: effect

tsdoc.summary:
Registers a function to run whenever its accessed signals change.

The function runs immediately when `effect()` is called, and re-runs
synchronously whenever any tracked signal's value changes. Dependencies
are established by accessing `.value` on signals during execution.
remarks:
- The function runs immediately when `effect()` is called
- Dependencies are only tracked for signals whose `.value` is accessed during execution
- If a signal is accessed conditionally and the condition is false on first run, it won't be tracked
- Effects run synchronously when dependencies change
- Disposal is lazy - effects are removed on the next signal update, not immediately

params.name: fn
params.description:
A function that should access `.value` on signals to establish
dependencies. Contains side effects (logging, DOM updates, etc.).

returns:
The input function augmented with `canDisposeNow` and `dispose()`
methods for cleanup
see: {@link dispose} - For disposing multiple effects or derived signals

core:dispose.name: dispose

tsdoc.summary:
Disposes multiple derived signals and/or effects at once.

This utility function calls `.dispose()` on each argument, stopping
dependency tracking for derived signals and marking effects for disposal.
remarks:
- Empty argument list is valid (no-op)
- Can mix derived signals and effects in the same call
- Disposing the same effect multiple times is safe (idempotent)
- For derived signals: stops dependency tracking
- For effects: marks for disposal (removed on next signal update)

params.name: derivedSignalsOrEffects
params.description:
Variable arguments of derived signals
and/or effects to dispose

see: {@link DerivedSignal.dispose} - For disposing individual derived signals

tsdoc.summary: Checks whether a value is a source signal.
remarks:
- Returns false for derived signals, non-signals, and plain values
- Returns false for `null` and `undefined`

params.name: input
params.description: Any value to check

returns: `true` if the value has `type: "source-signal"`, `false` otherwise
see: {@link valueIsSignal} - For checking any signal type

tsdoc.summary: Checks whether a value is a derived signal.
remarks:
- Returns false for source signals, non-signals, and plain values
- Returns false for `null` and `undefined`

params.name: input
params.description: Any value to check

returns: `true` if the value has `type: "derived-signal"`, `false` otherwise
see: {@link valueIsSignal} - For checking any signal type

tsdoc.summary: Checks whether a value is any signal (source or derived).
remarks:
- Returns true for both source and derived signals
- Returns false for non-signals and plain values
- Returns false for `null` and `undefined`

params.name: input
params.description: Any value to check

returns: `true` if the value is a source or derived signal, `false` otherwise
see: {@link Signal} - For the signal union type

core:valueIsNonSignalObject.name: valueIsNonSignalObject

tsdoc.summary: Checks whether a value is a non-signal object, optionally matching specific types.
remarks:
- Empty types array is treated as no type restriction
- Returns false for `null` and `undefined`
examples:
```typescript
const nonSig = getNonSignalObject(42);
valueIsNonSignalObject(nonSig); // true
valueIsNonSignalObject(nonSig, ["number"]); // true
valueIsNonSignalObject(nonSig, ["string"]); // false
```

params.name: input
params.description: Any value to check

params.name: shouldMatchAnyOfTypes
params.description:
Optional array of primitive type names to match
(for example, `["string", "number"]`)

returns:
`true` if the value has `type: "non-signal"` and (if types provided)
the value matches one of the types

core:valueIsSignalifiedObject.name: valueIsSignalifiedObject
core:valueIsSignalifiedObject.signature:
export const valueIsSignalifiedObject = (input: any): boolean =>
  valueIsSignal(input) || valueIsNonSignalObject(input);

tsdoc.summary: Checks whether a value is a signal or non-signal object.
remarks:
- Returns true for source signals, derived signals, and non-signal objects
- Returns false for plain values
- Returns false for `null` and `undefined`

params.name: input
params.description: Any value to check

returns: `true` if the value is a signal or non-signal object, `false` otherwise
see: {@link SignalifiedObject} - For the signalified object union type

core:valueIsNonSignalString.name: valueIsNonSignalString
core:valueIsNonSignalString.signature:
export const valueIsNonSignalString = (input: any): boolean =>
  valueIsNonSignalObject(input, ["string"]);

tsdoc.summary: Checks whether a value is a non-signal of type string.
remarks: - Returns false for plain strings (not wrapped in non-signal)

params.name: input
params.description: Any value to check

returns: `true` if the value is a non-signal with a string value, `false` otherwise
see: {@link valueIsNonSignalObject} - For the general non-signal checker

core:valueIsNonSignalStringArray.name: valueIsNonSignalStringArray
core:valueIsNonSignalStringArray.signature:
export const valueIsNonSignalStringArray = (input: any): boolean =>
  input?.type === "non-signal" &&
  Array.isArray(input?.value) &&
  (input?.value as any[]).every((item) => typeof item === "string");

tsdoc.summary: Checks whether a value is a non-signal of type string array.
remarks:
- Checks that all array elements are strings
- Returns false for empty arrays
- Returns false for arrays with non-string elements

params.name: input
params.description: Any value to check

returns: `true` if the value is a non-signal with a string array value, `false` otherwise
see: {@link valueIsNonSignalObject} - For the general non-signal checker

core:valueIsMaybeSignalValueOfStringOrAr.name: valueIsMaybeSignalValueOfStringOrArray
core:valueIsMaybeSignalValueOfStringOrAr.signature:
export const valueIsMaybeSignalValueOfStringOrArray = (input: any): boolean =>
  typeof value(input) === "string" || Array.isArray(value(input));

tsdoc.summary: Checks whether a value, after unwrapping, is a string or array.
remarks:
- Unwraps signals and non-signals to get the plain value
- Returns true if the plain value is a string or array
- Returns false for other types
- Returns false for `null` and `undefined`

params.name: input
params.description: Any value to check

returns: `true` if the unwrapped value is a string or array, `false` otherwise

core:value.name: value
core:value.signature:
export const value = <T>(input: MaybeSignalValue<T>): T =>
  valueIsSignalifiedObject(input)
    ? ((input as SignalifiedObject<T>).value as T)
    : (input as T);

tsdoc.summary:
Extracts the plain value from a signal, non-signal, or plain value.

This utility function unwraps signalified objects to get their underlying
plain values. If the input is already a plain value, it returns it as-is.
remarks:
- Does not trigger dependency tracking
- Works with `null` and `undefined`
- Works with nested structures
examples:
```typescript
const count = signal(42);
const nonSig = getNonSignalObject("hello");

value(count); // 42
value(nonSig); // "hello"
value(100); // 100
```

params.name: input
params.description: A signal, non-signal, or plain value

returns: The unwrapped plain value

template.name: T
template.description: The type of the plain value

core:getDesignalifiedMethodParams.name: getDesignalifiedMethodParams

tsdoc.summary:
Converts signalified method parameters to plain values.

This helper function is used by trap methods to unwrap parameters that may be
signals, plain values, or non-signals into their plain values before passing
them to the underlying JavaScript methods (e.g., string methods, array methods).

This allows trap methods to accept signalified parameters while still being
able to call standard JavaScript methods that expect plain values.

params.name: methodParams
params.description: Signalified parameters (signals, plain values, or non-signals)

returns: Array of plain values extracted from the signalified parameters

tsdoc.summary: A union type representing either a source or derived signal.

template.name: T
template.description: The type of value the signal holds

tsdoc.summary: A union type representing either a non-signal object or a plain value.

A union type representing a signal or a plain value.

This is commonly used for function parameters that can accept either
a signal or a plain value of the same type.
A union type representing a source signal or a plain value.
A union type representing a derived signal or a plain value.
A union type representing a signalified object (signal or non-signal).

core:MaybeSignalValue.name: MaybeSignalValue
core:MaybeSignalValue.signature: export type MaybeSignalValue<T> = T | NonSignal<T> | Signal<T>;

tsdoc.summary:
A union type representing a signal, non-signal, or plain value.

This is the most permissive type for values that may or may not be signalified.

core:NonNullSignalValue.name: NonNullSignalValue
core:NonNullSignalValue.signature:
export type NonNullSignalValue<S> = S extends null | undefined
  ? never
  : S extends SourceSignal<infer SS | null | undefined>
  ? SourceSignal<SS>
  : S extends DerivedSignal<infer DS | null | undefined>
  ? DerivedSignal<DS>
  : S extends NonSignal<infer NS | null | undefined>
  ? NonSignal<NS>
  : S;

tsdoc.summary:
A utility type that removes `null` and `undefined` from signal realm types.

This is similar to TypeScript's `NonNullable` but handles signal types specifically.

core:MaybeSignalValues.name: MaybeSignalValues

tsdoc.summary:
Converts a tuple type to a tuple of `MaybeSignalValue` types.

Functions are left as-is, while other values are converted to MaybeSignalValue.

core:PlainValues.name: PlainValues

tsdoc.summary:
Extracts plain values from a `MaybeSignalValues` tuple.

This is the inverse of MaybeSignalValues, converting signalified values
back to their plain types.

template.name: T
template.description: The MaybeSignalValues tuple to extract from

core:PlainValue.name: PlainValue
core:PlainValue.signature:
export type PlainValue<I extends MaybeSignalValue<unknown>> =
  I extends SignalifiedObject<infer T> ? T : I;

tsdoc.summary:
Extracts a plain value from a `MaybeSignalValue`.

If the input is a signalified object, returns the wrapped value.
Otherwise, returns the input as-is.

core:DerivedSignal.name: DerivedSignal
core:DerivedSignal.signature:
export type DerivedSignal<T> = BaseDerivedSignal<T> &
  (T extends any[]
    ? ArraySignalNonMutatingMethodsObject<T>
    : T extends Record<string, any>
      ? ObjectSignalNonMutatingMethodsObject<T>
      : T extends string
        ? StringSignalNonMutatingMethodsObject
        : T extends number
          ? NumberSignalNonMutatingMethodsObject
          : T extends boolean
            ? BooleanSignalNonMutatingMethodsObject
            : {});

tsdoc.summary:
A read-only derived signal computed from other signals.

Derived signals automatically recompute their value whenever any of their
tracked dependencies change. Dependencies are established by accessing
`.value` on signals during the initial computation.
remarks:
- The value getter function receives the previous computed value (undefined on first run)
- Dependencies are only tracked for signals whose `.value` is accessed during execution
- If a signal is accessed conditionally and the condition is false on first run, it won't be tracked
- Calling `dispose()` stops the derived signal from tracking dependencies
- After disposal, the value remains accessible but won't update
- Array derived signals get non-mutating array methods (map, filter, etc.)
- String derived signals get non-mutating string methods (toLowerCase, toUpperCase, etc.)
- Number derived signals get non-mutating number methods (toFixed, toPrecision, etc.)
- Boolean derived signals get non-mutating boolean methods (not, toString)
see: {@link signal} - For creating mutable source signals
{@link effect} - For registering functions to run when signal values change

template.name: T
template.description: The type of value the derived signal holds

core:DerivedValueGetterWithSignals.name: DerivedValueGetterWithSignals
core:DerivedValueGetterWithSignals.signature: export type DerivedValueGetterWithSignals<T> = (oldValue: T | undefined) => T;

tsdoc.summary:
A function that computes a derived signal's value.

This function receives the previous computed value and should access
`.value` on signals to establish dependencies. The function is called
whenever any tracked dependency changes.
remarks:
- Always access signals via `.value` to establish dependencies
- If a signal is accessed conditionally and the condition is false on first run, it won't be tracked
- The previous value is the previous RETURN value, not the previous dependency values

params.name: oldValue
params.description: The previous computed value (undefined on first run)

returns: The new computed value

template.name: T
template.description: The type of value to return

core:derive.name: derive

tsdoc.summary:
Creates a read-only derived signal computed from other signals.

The derived signal's value is computed by the provided function, which
should access `.value` on signals to establish dependencies. The value
is recomputed whenever any tracked dependency changes.
remarks:
- Dependencies are only tracked for signals whose `.value` is accessed during execution
- If a signal is accessed conditionally and the condition is false on first run, it won't be tracked
- The previous value is undefined on the first computation
- Derived signals can depend on other derived signals (chaining)

params.name: valueGetterFn
params.description:
A function that computes the derived value.
Receives the previous computed value (undefined on first run).

returns: A derived signal with `value`, `prevValue`, and `dispose()` methods
see: {@link DerivedValueGetterWithSignals} - The type of the value getter function
{@link signal} - For creating mutable source signals
{@link effect} - For registering functions to run when signal values change

template.name: T
template.description: The type of value the derived signal holds

core:NonSignal.name: NonSignal

tsdoc.summary:
A runtime type wrapper for plain values.

NonSignal objects are used for runtime type discrimination in complex
type scenarios where TypeScript's compile-time types are insufficient.
They enable distinguishing between plain values and signalified objects
at runtime.
remarks:
- Used with `MaybeSignal` types to resolve ambiguity at runtime
- Has a `type: "non-signal"` property for runtime type checking
- The `value` property holds the wrapped plain value
see: {@link MaybeSignal} - For union types that include signals

core:getNonSignalObject.name: getNonSignalObject

tsdoc.summary:
Wraps a plain value in a NonSignal object for runtime type discrimination.

This function is useful when you need to explicitly mark a value as a
non-signal for runtime type checking in complex type scenarios.
remarks:
- Used for runtime type checking in complex type scenarios
- Enables distinguishing between plain values and signalified objects
- The wrapped value can be any JavaScript type

params.name: input
params.description: Any JavaScript value to wrap

returns: A NonSignal object with the wrapped value
see: {@link valueIsNonSignalObject} - For checking if a value is a NonSignal

template.name: T
template.description: The type of value to wrap

core:setCurrentEffect.name: setCurrentEffect
core:setCurrentEffect.signature:
export const setCurrentEffect = (effect: SignalsEffect | null) =>
  (_currentSignalEffect = effect);

tsdoc.summary:
Sets the currently executing effect.

Called by the effect function before running the user's function to enable
automatic dependency tracking. After the effect completes, this is set to null.

params.name: effect
params.description: The effect to set as current, or `null` to clear tracking

core:signal.name: signal

tsdoc.summary:
Creates a mutable source signal from any JavaScript value.

A signal is a reactive data unit that automatically notifies dependent
computations when its value changes. Signals use a global variable-based
dependency tracking system to establish relationships with effects.
remarks:
- Setting the same value does not trigger effects
- Effects are triggered synchronously and immediately upon value change
- Signal values are stored immutably via `@cyftec/immut`
- Object `set()` performs a shallow merge
- Array mutation methods create new arrays internally

params.name: initialValue
params.description: Any JavaScript value to convert to a signal

returns:
A source signal with a `value` getter/setter. Arrays include
mutation methods and plain objects include `set()`.
see: {@link effect} - For registering functions to run when signal values change
{@link derive} - For creating read-only derived signals

template.name: T
template.description: The type of value the signal holds

core:BaseSourceSignal.name: BaseSourceSignal

tsdoc.summary: Base source signal type with value getter/setter.

template.name: T
template.description: The type of value the signal holds

core:BaseDerivedSignal.name: BaseDerivedSignal

tsdoc.summary:
Base derived signal type with read-only value access.

Derived signals are computed from other signals and automatically update
when their dependencies change.
remarks:
- Value is read-only (computed from dependencies)
- The `prevValue` getter provides access to the previous computed value
- Calling `dispose()` stops the signal from tracking its dependencies

template.name: T
template.description: The type of value the signal holds

tsdoc.summary: Base signal type union for both source and derived signals.
remarks:
- Source signals have mutable values via the setter
- Derived signals have read-only values computed from dependencies

template.name: T
template.description: The type of value the signal holds

core:SignalsEffect.name: SignalsEffect

tsdoc.summary:
A function that can be registered to run when signal values change.

Effects are created by the `effect()` function and track dependencies
by accessing `.value` on signals during execution.
remarks:
- The effect function runs immediately when created
- It re-runs whenever any tracked signal's value changes
- The `canDisposeNow` flag marks the effect for disposal
- Calling `dispose()` sets `canDisposeNow` to true

core:ArraySignalIntrinsicMutatingMethods.name: ArraySignalIntrinsicMutatingMethodsObject

tsdoc.summary:
Intrinsic mutating methods for array signals.

These methods mirror JavaScript Array mutating methods but internally create
new immutable arrays and trigger effects.
remarks:
- All methods create new arrays internally
- Effects are triggered synchronously
- Methods expose a mutable-style API while maintaining immutability

core:ArraySignalCustomMutatingMethodsObj.name: ArraySignalCustomMutatingMethodsObject

tsdoc.summary:
Custom mutating methods for array signals.

These are library-specific methods that provide additional functionality
beyond JavaScript's intrinsic array methods.
remarks:
- `keep()` is the inverse of `filter()` - keeps items matching the predicate
- `remove()` deletes items matching the predicate

tsdoc.summary:
Combined mutating methods for array signals.

Combines intrinsic and custom mutating methods into a single type.

core:ArraySignalIntrinsicNonMutatingMeth.name: ArraySignalIntrinsicNonMutatingMethodsObject

tsdoc.summary:
Intrinsic non-mutating methods for array signals.

These methods mirror JavaScript Array non-mutating methods but return
derived signals instead of plain values.
remarks:
- All methods return derived signals
- Methods are reactive and update when the source array changes
- Works with both source and derived signals

core:ArraySignalCustomNonMutatingMethods.name: ArraySignalCustomNonMutatingMethodsObject

tsdoc.summary:
Custom non-mutating methods for array signals.

These are library-specific methods that provide additional functionality
beyond JavaScript's intrinsic array methods.
remarks:
- `lastItem` returns a derived signal for the last array element
- `partition` splits an array into two derived signals based on a predicate

tsdoc.summary:
Combined non-mutating methods for array signals.

Combines intrinsic and custom non-mutating methods into a single type.

core:ArraySourceSignalMethodsObject.name: ArraySourceSignalMethodsObject
core:ArraySourceSignalMethodsObject.signature:
export type ArraySourceSignalMethodsObject<T extends any[]> =
  ArraySignalMutatingMethodsObject<T> & ArraySignalNonMutatingMethodsObject<T>;

tsdoc.summary:
Combined methods for array source signals.

Combines mutating and non-mutating methods for array source signals.
remarks:
- Mutating methods trigger effects synchronously
- Non-mutating methods return derived signals
- Methods create new arrays internally but feel mutable

core:ArraySourceSignal.name: ArraySourceSignal
core:ArraySourceSignal.signature:
export type ArraySourceSignal<T extends any[]> = BaseSourceSignal<T> &
  ArraySourceSignalMethodsObject<T>;

tsdoc.summary:
Source signal for arrays with mutation and non-mutating methods.

Array source signals include both mutating methods (push, pop, splice, etc.)
and non-mutating methods (map, filter, etc.) that return derived signals.

core:ObjectSourceSignalMutatingMethodsOb.name: ObjectSourceSignalMutatingMethodsObject

tsdoc.summary: Mutating methods for object signals.
remarks:
- `set()` performs a shallow merge with the current value
- Triggers effects synchronously

core:ObjectSignalNonMutatingMethodsObjec.name: ObjectSignalNonMutatingMethodsObject

tsdoc.summary: Non-mutating methods for object signals.

core:ObjectSourceSignalMethodsObject.name: ObjectSourceSignalMethodsObject

tsdoc.summary: Returns the object's keys as a derived signal.

core:ObjectSourceSignal.name: ObjectSourceSignal
core:ObjectSourceSignal.signature:
export type ObjectSourceSignal<T extends Record<string, any>> =
  BaseSourceSignal<T> & ObjectSourceSignalMethodsObject<T>;

tsdoc.summary:
Source signal for plain objects with partial update method.

Object source signals include the `set()` method for partial updates and
non-mutating methods for accessing properties as derived signals.

core:StringSignalIntrinsicNonMutatingMet.name: StringSignalIntrinsicNonMutatingMethodsObject

tsdoc.summary:
Intrinsic non-mutating methods for string signals.

These methods mirror JavaScript String non-mutating methods but return
derived signals instead of plain values.
remarks:
- All methods return derived signals
- Methods are reactive and update when the source string changes
- Works with both source and derived signals

core:StringSignalCustomNonMutatingMethod.name: StringSignalCustomNonMutatingMethodsObject

tsdoc.summary:
Custom non-mutating methods for string signals.

These are library-specific methods that provide additional functionality
beyond JavaScript's intrinsic string methods.
remarks:
- `lowercase` returns a derived signal for the lowercase version
- `Sentencecase` returns a derived signal with first letter capitalized
- `TitleCase` returns a derived signal with each word capitalized
- `UPPERCASE` returns a derived signal for the uppercase version

tsdoc.summary:
Combined non-mutating methods for string signals.

Combines intrinsic and custom non-mutating methods into a single type.

Source signal for strings with non-mutating methods.

String source signals include non-mutating methods that return derived signals.

core:NumberSignalIntrinsicNonMutatingMet.name: NumberSignalIntrinsicNonMutatingMethodsObject

tsdoc.summary:
Intrinsic non-mutating methods for number signals.

These methods mirror JavaScript Number non-mutating methods but return
derived signals instead of plain values.
remarks:
- All methods return derived signals
- Methods are reactive and update when the source number changes
- Works with both source and derived signals

core:NumberSignalCustomNonMutatingMethod.name: NumberSignalCustomNonMutatingMethodsObject

tsdoc.summary:
Custom non-mutating methods for number signals.

These are library-specific methods that provide additional functionality
beyond JavaScript's intrinsic number methods.
remarks: - `toConfined` confines the number within a range

tsdoc.summary:
Combined non-mutating methods for number signals.

Combines intrinsic and custom non-mutating methods into a single type.

Source signal for numbers with non-mutating methods.

Number source signals include non-mutating methods that return derived signals.
Mutating methods for boolean signals.
remarks:
- `toggle()` flips the boolean value
- Triggers effects synchronously

core:BooleanSignalNonMutatingMethodsObje.name: BooleanSignalNonMutatingMethodsObject

tsdoc.summary:
Custom non-mutating methods for boolean signals.

These are library-specific methods that provide additional functionality
for boolean values.
remarks: - `negated` returns the negated boolean value

tsdoc.summary: Negated boolean value.

Source signal for booleans with non-mutating methods.

Boolean source signals include non-mutating methods that return derived signals.

core:SourceSignal.name: SourceSignal
core:SourceSignal.signature:
export type SourceSignal<T> = T extends any[]
  ? ArraySourceSignal<T>
  : T extends Record<string, any>
    ? ObjectSourceSignal<T>
    : T extends string
      ? StringSourceSignal
      : T extends number
        ? NumberSourceSignal
        : T extends boolean
          ? BooleanSourceSignal
          : BaseSourceSignal<T>;

tsdoc.summary:
A mutable source signal created from plain JavaScript data.

Source signals can notify dependent computations when their value changes.
The specific type (array, object, string, number, or boolean) determines which
additional methods are available.
remarks:
- For arrays: includes array mutation methods (push, pop, splice, etc.)
- For plain objects: includes `set()` method for partial updates
- For strings: includes string methods (toLowerCase, toUpperCase, etc.)
- For numbers: includes number methods (toFixed, toPrecision, etc.)
- For booleans: includes boolean methods (not, toString)
- For other primitives: only the base signal interface

template.name: T
template.description: The type of value the signal holds

core:getArraySignalIntrinsicMutatingMeth.name: getArraySignalIntrinsicMutatingMethodsObject

tsdoc.summary:
Creates intrinsic mutating methods for array signals.

These methods mirror JavaScript Array mutating methods but internally create
new immutable arrays and trigger effects.
remarks:
- All methods create new arrays internally using `Array.from()`
- Effects are triggered synchronously
- Methods expose a mutable-style API while maintaining immutability

params.name: valueSetter
params.description: Updates the signal value and triggers effects

returns: Intrinsic mutating methods for array signals

core:getArraySignalCustomMutatingMethods.name: getArraySignalCustomMutatingMethodsObject

tsdoc.summary:
Creates custom mutating methods for array signals.

These are library-specific methods that provide additional functionality
beyond JavaScript's intrinsic array methods.
remarks:
- `keep()` is the inverse of `filter()` - keeps items matching the predicate
- `remove()` deletes items matching the predicate

params.name: valueSetter
params.description: Updates the signal value and triggers effects

returns: Custom mutating methods for array signals

core:getArraySignalMutatingMethodsObject.name: getArraySignalMutatingMethodsObject

tsdoc.summary:
Creates combined mutating methods for array signals.

Combines intrinsic and custom mutating methods into a single object.

params.name: valueSetter
params.description: Updates the signal value and triggers effects

returns: Combined mutating methods for array signals

core:getArraySignalIntrinsicNonMutatingM.name: getArraySignalIntrinsicNonMutatingMethodsObject
core:getArraySignalIntrinsicNonMutatingM.signature:
export const getArraySignalIntrinsicNonMutatingMethodsObject = <
  T extends any[],
>(
  baseArraySignal: BaseSignal<T>,
): ArraySignalIntrinsicNonMutatingMethodsObject<T> => {
  return {
    at: (...args: Parameters<Array<T[number]>["at"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseArraySignal.value.at(...args)),
    concat: (...args: Parameters<Array<T[number]>["concat"]>) =>
      derive(
        () => baseArraySignal.value.concat(...(args as any)) as T[number][],
      ),
    every: (...args: Parameters<Array<T[number]>["every"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseArraySignal.value.every(...args)),
    filter: (...args: Parameters<Array<T[number]>["filter"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseArraySignal.value.filter(...args) as T[number][]),
    find: (...args: Parameters<Array<T[number]>["find"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseArraySignal.value.find(...args)),
    findIndex: (...args: Parameters<Array<T[number]>["findIndex"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseArraySignal.value.findIndex(...args)),
    findLast: (...args: Parameters<Array<T[number]>["findLast"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseArraySignal.value.findLast(...args)),
    findLastIndex: (...args: Parameters<Array<T[number]>["findLastIndex"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseArraySignal.value.findLastIndex(...args)),
    length: () => derive(() => baseArraySignal.value.length),
    map: <U>(mapFn: (item: T[number], index: number, array: T) => U) =>
      derive(() => baseArraySignal.value.map(mapFn as any) as U[]),
    reduce: <U>(
      reducerFn: (
        previousValue: U,
        currentValue: T[number],
        currentIndex: number,
        array: T,
      ) => U,
      initialValue: U,
    ) =>
      derive(
        () => baseArraySignal.value.reduce(reducerFn as any, initialValue) as U,
      ),
    reduceRight: <U>(
      reducerFn: (
        previousValue: U,
        currentValue: T[number],
        currentIndex: number,
        array: T,
      ) => U,
      initialValue: U,
    ) =>
      derive(
        () =>
          baseArraySignal.value.reduceRight(
            reducerFn as any,
            initialValue,
          ) as U,
      ),
    some: (...args: Parameters<Array<T[number]>["some"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseArraySignal.value.some(...args)),
    toReversed: (...args: Parameters<Array<T[number]>["toReversed"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseArraySignal.value.toReversed(...args) as T[number][]),
    toSorted: (...args: Parameters<Array<T[number]>["toSorted"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseArraySignal.value.toSorted(...args) as T[number][]),
    toSpliced: (...args: Parameters<Array<T[number]>["toSpliced"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseArraySignal.value.toSpliced(...args) as T[number][]),
  };
};

tsdoc.summary:
Creates intrinsic non-mutating methods for array signals.

These methods mirror JavaScript Array non-mutating methods but return
derived signals instead of plain values.
remarks:
- All methods return derived signals
- Methods are reactive and update when the source array changes
- Works with both source and derived signals

params.name: baseArraySignal
params.description: The base array signal to access values from

returns: Intrinsic non-mutating methods for array signals

core:getArraySignalCustomNonMutatingMeth.name: getArraySignalCustomNonMutatingMethodsObject

tsdoc.summary:
Creates custom non-mutating methods for array signals.

These are library-specific methods that provide additional functionality
beyond JavaScript's intrinsic array methods.
remarks:
- `lastItem` returns a derived signal for the last array element
- `partition` splits an array into two derived signals based on a predicate

params.name: baseArraySignal
params.description: The base array signal to access values from

returns: Custom non-mutating methods for array signals

core:getArraySignalNonMutatingMethodsObj.name: getArraySignalNonMutatingMethodsObject

tsdoc.summary:
Creates combined non-mutating methods for array signals.

Combines intrinsic and custom non-mutating methods into a single object.
remarks:
- All methods return derived signals
- Works with both source and derived signals
- Methods are reactive and update when the source array changes

params.name: baseArraySignal
params.description: The base array signal to access values from

returns: Combined non-mutating methods for array signals

core:getArraySourceSignalMethodsObject.name: getArraySourceSignalMethodsObject

tsdoc.summary:
Creates combined methods for array source signals.

Combines mutating and non-mutating methods for array source signals.
remarks:
- Non-mutating methods return derived signals
- Mutating methods create new arrays internally but feel mutable

params.name: valueSetter
params.description: Updates the signal value and triggers effects

params.name: baseArraySignal
params.description: The base array signal to access values from

returns: Combined methods for array source signals

core:getStringSignalIntrinsicNonMutating.name: getStringSignalIntrinsicNonMutatingMethodsObject
core:getStringSignalIntrinsicNonMutating.signature:
export const getStringSignalIntrinsicNonMutatingMethodsObject = (
  baseStringSignal: BaseSignal<string>,
): StringSignalIntrinsicNonMutatingMethodsObject => {
  return {
    at: (...args: Parameters<String["at"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseStringSignal.value.at(...args)),
    charAt: (...args: Parameters<String["charAt"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseStringSignal.value.charAt(...args)),
    charCodeAt: (...args: Parameters<String["charCodeAt"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseStringSignal.value.charCodeAt(...args)),
    codePointAt: (...args: Parameters<String["codePointAt"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseStringSignal.value.codePointAt(...args)),
    concat: (...args: Parameters<String["concat"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseStringSignal.value.concat(...args)),
    endsWith: (...args: Parameters<String["endsWith"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseStringSignal.value.endsWith(...args)),
    includes: (...args: Parameters<String["includes"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseStringSignal.value.includes(...args)),
    indexOf: (...args: Parameters<String["indexOf"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseStringSignal.value.indexOf(...args)),
    lastIndexOf: (...args: Parameters<String["lastIndexOf"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseStringSignal.value.lastIndexOf(...args)),
    padEnd: (...args: Parameters<String["padEnd"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseStringSignal.value.padEnd(...args)),
    padStart: (...args: Parameters<String["padStart"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseStringSignal.value.padStart(...args)),
    repeat: (...args: Parameters<String["repeat"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseStringSignal.value.repeat(...args)),
    slice: (...args: Parameters<String["slice"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseStringSignal.value.slice(...args)),
    startsWith: (...args: Parameters<String["startsWith"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseStringSignal.value.startsWith(...args)),
    substring: (...args: Parameters<String["substring"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseStringSignal.value.substring(...args)),
    trim: (...args: Parameters<String["trim"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseStringSignal.value.trim(...args)),
    trimEnd: (...args: Parameters<String["trimEnd"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseStringSignal.value.trimEnd(...args)),
    trimStart: (...args: Parameters<String["trimStart"]>) =>
      derive(() => baseStringSignal.value.trimStart(...args)),
    length: () => derive(() => baseStringSignal.value.length),
    localeCompare: (
      that: string,
      locales?: string | string[] | undefined,
      options?: Intl.CollatorOptions,
    ) =>
      derive(() =>
        baseStringSignal.value.localeCompare(that, locales, options),
      ),
    normalize: (form: "NFC" | "NFD" | "NFKC" | "NFKD") =>
      derive(() => baseStringSignal.value.normalize(form)),
    replace: (searchValue: string | RegExp, replaceValue: string) =>
      derive(() => baseStringSignal.value.replace(searchValue, replaceValue)),
    replaceAll: (searchValue: string | RegExp, replaceValue: string) =>
      derive(() =>
        baseStringSignal.value.replaceAll(searchValue, replaceValue),
      ),
    search: (regexp: RegExp) =>
      derive(() => baseStringSignal.value.search(regexp)),
    split: (separator: string | RegExp, limit?: number | undefined) =>
      derive(() => baseStringSignal.value.split(separator, limit)),
    toLocaleLowerCase: (locales?: string | string[] | undefined) =>
      derive(() => baseStringSignal.value.toLocaleLowerCase(locales)),
    toLocaleUpperCase: (locales?: string | string[] | undefined) =>
      derive(() => baseStringSignal.value.toLocaleUpperCase(locales)),
  };
};

tsdoc.summary:
Creates intrinsic non-mutating methods for string signals.

These methods mirror JavaScript String non-mutating methods but return
derived signals instead of plain values.
remarks:
- All methods return derived signals
- Methods are reactive and update when the source string changes
- Works with both source and derived signals
- Methods are lazy - derived signals are only created when accessed

params.name: baseStringSignal
params.description: The base string signal to access values from

returns: Intrinsic non-mutating methods for string signals

core:getStringSignalCustomNonMutatingMet.name: getStringSignalCustomNonMutatingMethodsObject

tsdoc.summary:
Creates custom non-mutating methods for string signals.

These are library-specific methods that provide additional functionality
beyond JavaScript's intrinsic string methods.
remarks:
- `lowercase` returns a derived signal for the lowercase version
- `Sentencecase` returns a derived signal with first letter capitalized
- `TitleCase` returns a derived signal with each word capitalized
- `UPPERCASE` returns a derived signal for the uppercase version
- Methods are lazy - derived signals are only created when accessed

params.name: baseStringSignal
params.description: The base string signal to access values from

returns: Custom non-mutating methods for string signals

core:getStringSignalMethodsObject.name: getStringSignalMethodsObject

tsdoc.summary:

Combines intrinsic and custom non-mutating methods into a single object.
remarks:
- All methods return derived signals
- Works with both source and derived signals
- Methods are reactive and update when the source string changes
- Methods are lazy - derived signals are only created when accessed

params.name: baseStringSignal
params.description: The base string signal to access values from

returns: Combined non-mutating methods for string signals

core:getObjectSignalMutatingMethodsObjec.name: getObjectSignalMutatingMethodsObject

tsdoc.summary: Returns an object with methods to update the source signal's value.
remarks:
- `set()` performs a shallow merge with the current value
- Works with both source and derived signals

returns: An object with methods to update the source signal's value

core:getObjectSignalNonMutatingMethodsOb.name: getObjectSignalNonMutatingMethodsObject

tsdoc.summary: Creates the object trap for signalified plain objects.
remarks:
- Throws if the input is not a plain object after unwrapping
- Property accessors are derived signals

params.name: input
params.description: A signalified plain object

returns: A record trap exposing derived property accessors

core:getObjectSourceSignalMethodsObject.name: getObjectSourceSignalMethodsObject

tsdoc.summary: Returns an object with methods to update the source signal's value.
remarks:
- `set()` performs a shallow merge with the current value
- Works with both source and derived signals

params.name: valueSetter
params.description: A function that updates the signal value and triggers effects

returns: An object with methods to update the source signal's value

api:compute.name: compute

tsdoc.summary:
Creates a derived signal from a function with signalified arguments.

This is a convenience wrapper around `derive` that automatically unwraps
signal arguments before passing them to the function. The derived signal
recomputes whenever any of the signalified arguments changes.
remarks:
- Works with functions of any arity
- Can mix signals and plain values in arguments
- Plain value arguments do not trigger recomputation
- Arguments are unwrapped using the `value()` helper

params.name: computerFn
params.description: A function that receives plain values and returns a value

params.name: restArgs
params.description: Signalified arguments matching the function parameters

returns: A derived signal of the function's return type
see: {@link derive} - For the underlying derived signal primitive

api:StringSignalDeriverTemplateExpressio.name: StringSignalDeriverTemplateExpressions

tsdoc.summary: The expressions allowed inside the template placeholders.
remarks:
- `Signal<any>` is accepted directly
- `DerivedValueGetterWithSignals<any>` is accepted as a deferred expression
- Plain values are accepted as-is

api:tmpl.name: tmpl

tsdoc.summary:
Tagged template function for string interpolation with signals.

Creates a derived signal of the interpolated string that recomputes whenever
any tracked expression changes.
remarks:
- Expressions can be signals, derived expression functions, or plain values
- `null` and `undefined` become empty strings
- Values are converted to strings via `.toString()`
- Works with any combination of supported expression types

params.name: strings
params.description: The static string parts of the template literal

params.name: tlExpressions
params.description: The dynamic expressions inside the placeholders

returns: A derived signal of the interpolated string
see: {@link DerivedSignal} - For the derived signal type
{@link derive} - For the underlying derived signal primitive

api:promstates.name: promstates

tsdoc.summary:
Creates promise state signals for async operations.

This function returns a tuple containing a promise runner function and three
derived signals that track the promise's state: result, error, and running status.
remarks:
- On success: `result` is updated and `error` is cleared
- On failure: `error` is updated and `result` preserves the previous successful result
- The `ultimately` callback runs in the finally block
- The promise can be run multiple times
- If no initial value is provided, the result signal starts as `undefined`
- If the promise fails multiple times, the last successful result is preserved

params.name: promiseFn
params.description: A promise-returning function

params.name: initialValue
params.description: Optional initial value for the result signal

params.name: ultimately
params.description: Optional callback to run in the promise's finally block

returns:
A tuple of:
- `runPromise`: Function to run the promise
- `result`: Derived signal of the promise result
- `error`: Derived signal of the promise error
- `isRunning`: Derived signal of whether the promise is running
see: {@link DerivedSignal} - For the derived signal type

template.name: R
template.description: The type of the promise result

template.name: Args
template.description: The type of the promise function's arguments

template.name: I
template.description: The type of the initial value

api:stringAndArrayOp.name: stringAndArrayOp

tsdoc.summary: Creates the string/array-specific operation chain for a value.
remarks:
- Used when the evaluated value is a string or array
- Methods return new operation objects for chaining

params.name: input
params.description: A signalified string/array or value-producing function

returns: A string-and-array operation object with length-based methods

template.name: T
template.description: The string or array value type

api:OperationResult.name: OperationResult

tsdoc.summary:
Base operation result getters.

These getters provide the final derived signal results of composed operations.

api:GenericOperation.name: GenericOperation

tsdoc.summary: Generic operation with logical operations available for all types.

api:NumberOperation.name: NumberOperation

tsdoc.summary:
Number operation with math operations and comparisons.

Extends `GenericOperation` with number-specific operations.

api:StringAndArrayOperation.name: StringAndArrayOperation

tsdoc.summary:
String and array operation with length-based operations.

Extends `GenericOperation` with length-specific operations for strings and arrays.

api:Operation.name: Operation
api:Operation.signature:
export type Operation<T> = T extends number
  ? NumberOperation
  : T extends string | unknown[]
  ? StringAndArrayOperation
  : GenericOperation;

tsdoc.summary: Union type for all operation types, determined by the value type.
remarks:
- Number values map to `NumberOperation`
- String or array values map to `StringAndArrayOperation`
- Other types map to `GenericOperation`

template.name: T
template.description: The type of value the operation works with

api:genericOp.name: genericOp

tsdoc.summary: Creates the generic operation chain for a value.
remarks:
- Used for non-numeric and non-string-or-array inputs
- Methods return new operation objects for chaining

params.name: input
params.description: A signalified value or value-producing function

returns: A generic operation object with composable logical methods

api:numberOp.name: numberOp

tsdoc.summary: Creates the number-specific operation chain for a value.
remarks:
- Used when the evaluated value is a number
- Methods return new operation objects for chaining

params.name: input
params.description: A signalified number or value-producing function

returns: A number operation object with math and comparison methods

api:op.name: op

tsdoc.summary:
Creates an operation object for composing logical and mathematical operations.

This function evaluates the input and dispatches to the appropriate operation
implementation based on the evaluated type:
- Number values → numberOp (provides math operations: add, sub, mul, div, etc.)
- String or Array values → stringAndArrayOp (provides length-based operations)
- Other types → genericOp (provides logical operations: or, and, equals, etc.)
remarks:
- The operation type is determined by the runtime type of the evaluated value
- If input is a function, it is called to get the value
- Type changes in the input signal are not reflected in the operation type
- Methods return new operation objects for chaining
- Final results are obtained via getters such as `truthy`, `falsy`, and `result`

params.name: input
params.description: A signal, plain value, or value-producing function

returns: A type-specific operation object with chainable methods
see: {@link Operation} - For the operation type union

template.name: T
template.description: The type of value the operation works with

api:receive.name: receive

tsdoc.summary:
Connects multiple transmitter signals to a single receiver signal.

Whenever any transmitter changes, the receiver is updated to match that
transmitter's current value. If more than one transmitter changes in the same
propagation chain, the final receiver value follows effect execution order.
remarks:
- Each transmitter gets its own effect that updates the receiver
- Transmitters can be source or derived signals
- Receiver must be a source signal
- Passing no transmitters returns an empty effects array
- The receiver remains independently mutable

params.name: receiver
params.description: A source signal that will receive updates

params.name: transmittors
params.description: Multiple signals (source or derived) of the same type

returns: Array of effects that can be disposed to disconnect the bindings
see: {@link transmit} - For broadcasting from one transmitter to multiple receivers
{@link effect} - For the underlying effect primitive

template.name: T
template.description: The type of value the signals hold

api:transmit.name: transmit

tsdoc.summary:
Broadcasts changes from one transmitter signal to multiple receiver signals.

When the transmitter changes, all receivers are updated synchronously to the
same value. Each receiver remains independently mutable.
remarks:
- A single effect manages all receiver updates
- Transmitter can be source or derived signal
- Receivers must be source signals
- Passing no receivers creates a no-op effect
- The order of receiver updates is not guaranteed

params.name: transmittor
params.description: A signal (source or derived) that broadcasts changes

params.name: receivers
params.description: Multiple source signals that will receive updates

returns: A single effect that can be disposed to disconnect the broadcast
see: {@link receive} - For connecting multiple transmitters to a receiver
{@link effect} - For the underlying effect primitive

template.name: T
template.description: The type of value the signals hold
